summary: 5 conditions defined: C1 (BERT baseline), C2 (SRA-softmax+KL), C3 (sparsemax unsupervised), C4 (sparsemax+MSE all-12 heads, primary), C5 (sparsemax top-6 heads, H3). Primary contrast: C4 vs C2 on ERASER comprehensiveness AOPC (5 seeds, bootstrap 95% CI, TOST for F1 equivalence). H4 adversarial swap uses trained checkpoints. W5a (HateBRXplain, 3 seeds) for NeurIPS venue fit. Proposition 1 (structural-zero faithfulness) required in manuscript. ~110 GPU-hours total.
critical_context: Primary contrast: C4 (sparsemax+MSE) vs C2 (SRA softmax+KL) on comprehensiveness AOPC
Loss: L_total = CrossEntropy + alpha * MSE(sparsemax(z), normalized_rationale_mask)
Sparsemax on final encoder layer (layer 11) only; all earlier layers retain softmax
Rationale target: majority-vote binary mask, L1-normalized to valid simplex point
Alignment weight alpha: grid {0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 1.0}; select on val comprehensiveness
5 seeds per condition; SLURM array jobs (1 GPU per task)
H4 adversarial swap: replace attention with uniform; measure KL(P_swap || P_orig); sparsemax should show >=2x larger KL
Proposition 1 proved: zero-weight token -> zero contribution to CLS -> comprehensiveness = 0 by construction
W5a requires multilingual backbone (xlm-roberta-base) for HateBRXplain
Gate N2 pre-check passed: all novelty claims are testable; no cyclic design
